critic.verdict: An exceptionally well-crafted Easy chart with flawless density progression and comfortable, highly intuitive foot flow.

issues.problem: The isolated 8th note transition (L to U) represents a minor physical and reading difficulty spike for an Easy-level player.
An isolated 8th note directly leading into a JUMP LR (D to L to JUMP) can be disorienting for beginners at this skill level.

critic.verdict: While the chart accurately syncs to the track's dreamy bridge, the drop suffers from extreme repetition and several highly awkward transitions that spike the difficulty past a Medium level.

issues.problem: The chorus repeatedly loops the exact same 'LR Jump -> U -> D -> L' 8th-note pattern. This is highly monotonous, tiring, and fails to capture the glitchy, dynamic details of the song's production.
Awkward transition from a single tap on L (68.673) directly into a LD jump (68.946), forcing an uncomfortable 'one-foot' jump since the L panel is shared.
Similarly to 68.946, transitioning from a tap on L (86.135) directly into a LD jump (86.408) disrupts natural foot flow.
Dense 16th-note streams containing rapid direction changes (like D->L->U->D and R->L->D->U) introduce crossovers and double-steps that are too physically complex for a standard Medium chart.
A rapid 16th-note transition from a single D tap (75.358) to a LR jump (75.494) creates a sudden, awkward physical spike.

critic.verdict: A fundamentally well-timed chart with good structure, but severely dragged down by an excessively repetitive chorus section and a few awkward transitions after jumps.

issues.problem: The exact same four-note sequence (JUMP LR -> tap U -> tap D -> tap L) repeats 14 times consecutively. This creates a monotonous gameplay loop that lacks creative expression and physical variety.
The transition from an asymmetric LD jump directly into a fast 16th stream starting on U forces either an awkward body spin or an unnatural double-step.
